user_message: Just came out of consumption trend with Alpine Ski House. Turn this into my brief and pull out the asks.

input_artifacts.content:
Meeting: consumption trend Discussion - Alpine Ski House
Date: 2024-03-13
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Brian Yamamoto (Alpine Ski House), Marcus Williams (Microsoft), Kevin (Alpine Ski House)

[00:02:00] Brian Yamamoto (Alpine Ski House): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on consumption trend from our last session.

[00:06:00] Marcus Williams (Microsoft): On the consumption trend front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around vendor dependencies.

[00:09:00] Brian Yamamoto (Alpine Ski House): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is partner engagement.

[00:14:00] Brian Yamamoto (Alpine Ski House): Our VP reviewed this and has concerns about integration complexity. Can we address those?

[00:18:00] Brian Yamamoto (Alpine Ski House): The timeline for technical validation is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:21:00] Marcus Williams (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is security compliance.

[00:23:00] Brian Yamamoto (Alpine Ski House): Our director reviewed this and has concerns about stakeholder alignment. Can we address those?

[00:27:00] Kevin (Alpine Ski House): On the consumption trend front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around timeline constraints.

handoffs.condition: solution sketch needed from discovery signals

required_artifacts: BANT signal extraction with evidence quotes

usability_anchors: Score 5: Complete, well-structured output matching the Discovery Call Transcript -> Opportunity Build template with all required artifacts present and actionable
Score 3: Output covers main requirements but missing one or two secondary artifacts or lacking specificity
Score 1: Output is generic, missing key artifacts, or fails to address the specific request
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct consumption trend decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
